section: 3.1 Total C and N to environment
text:
3.1 Total C and N to environment


Prepare combined C and N data for plotting
farm_ends_CN <- rbind(farm_ends_carbon, farm_ends_nitrogen) %>%
  mutate(
    feed_level = factor(
      feed_level, 
      levels = c("min", "mean", "max"), 
      labels = c("Minimum", "Mean", "Maximum"))
  )

nutrient_labels <- data.frame(
  nutrient = levels(farm_ends_CN$nutrient),
  label = c("Carbon", "Nitrogen")
)


Figure 1 shows total carbon and nitrogen loading to the environment across different feeds and digestibility levels.


Plot total C and N loading to environment by feed and digestibility
p1 <- farm_ends_CN %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = feed_level, y = drop_units(mean_value_perbiom), fill = feed)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = feed_pal) +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(nutrient), 
    scales = "free"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = nutrient_labels, 
    aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
    hjust = -0.1, vjust = 1,
    size = 5.5, family = "serif",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Feed digestibility",
    y = expression("Nutrient loading (kg"~t[fish]^{-1}*")")
  ) +
  prettyplot_600() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none", 
    strip.text = element_blank(), 
    strip.background = element_blank()
  )

legend_grob <- ggpubr::get_legend(
  p1 +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 1)) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3),
      legend.margin     = margin(5, 6, 5, 6),
      legend.key.size   = unit(0.4, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title      = element_blank()
    )
)

p1 + inset_element(
  legend_grob,
  left   = 0.85, right  = 0.95,  # x start, x end (fraction of plot width)
  bottom = 0.85, top    = 0.95,   # y start, y end
  align_to = "full"  # relative to full plot, not just panel area
)








Figure 1: Total carbon (a) and nitrogen (b) loading to the environment with different feeds and digestibility levels.

3.2 Global loading of C and N


Prepare global C and N loading data for mapping
global_data <- rbind(farm_ends_carbon, farm_ends_nitrogen) %>% 
  filter(feed_level == "mean" & feed == "MD") %>% 
  merge(farm_locs, by = "farm_ID") %>% 
  st_as_sf(crs = 4326) %>% 
  st_transform(crs = "+proj=robin") %>% 
  select(farm_ID, feed, measure, mean_value_perbiom, nutrient, feed_level, iso3c, fao_code, country, region)

Cr <- range(global_data$mean_value_perbiom[global_data$nutrient == "C"])
Nr <- range(global_data$mean_value_perbiom[global_data$nutrient == "N"])

print(
  sprintf("Range of C: %f - %f Mt, range of N: %f - %f Mt", Cr[1], Cr[2], Nr[1], Nr[2])
)


[1] "Range of C: 52.856363 - 80.645071 Mt, range of N: 6.690431 - 10.209100 Mt"


Figure 2 maps the global distribution of total carbon and nitrogen loading for the marine-dominant feed at mean digestibility.


Map global C and N loading for marine-dominant feed at mean digestibility
p1 <- p_bigmap_robinson +
  geom_sf(
    data = filter(global_data, nutrient == "C"), 
    aes(color = drop_units(mean_value_perbiom), geometry = geometry), 
    size = 2
  ) +
  coord_sf() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "turbo", breaks = seq(50, 80, 5), limits = c(50, 81)) + 
  guides(color = guide_colourbar(
    title = expression(atop("C loading", paste("(kg"~t[fish]^{-1}*")"))),
    direction = "vertical", position = "right",
    label.position = "right", title.position = "top",
    title.vjust = 1, title.hjust = 0.5,
    frame.colour = "black", ticks.colour = "black",ticks.linewidth = 1,
    barwidth = 1.5, barheight = 15,
  )) +
  annotate(
    "text", label = "Carbon",
    x = -Inf, y = Inf, 
    vjust = 1, hjust = 0,
    size = 5.5, family = "serif",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
  labs(y = "Latitude", x = "Longitude") +
  prettyplot_600() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  remove_map_axes()

p2 <- p_bigmap_robinson +
  geom_sf(
    data = filter(global_data, nutrient == "N"), 
    aes(color = drop_units(mean_value_perbiom), geometry = geometry), 
    size = 2
  ) +
  coord_sf() +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0,0)) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "turbo", breaks = seq(6, 10, 1), limits = c(6, 10.25)) + 
  guides(color = guide_colourbar(
    title = expression(atop("N loading", paste("(kg"~t[fish]^{-1}*")"))),
    direction = "vertical", position = "right",
    label.position = "right", title.position = "top",
    title.vjust = 1, title.hjust = 0.5,
    frame.colour = "black", ticks.colour = "black",ticks.linewidth = 1,
    barwidth = 1.5, barheight = 12,
  )) +
  annotate(
    "text", label = "Nitrogen",
    x = -Inf, y = Inf, 
    vjust = 1, hjust = 0,
    size = 5.5, family = "serif",
    inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
  labs(y = "Latitude", x = "Longitude") +
  prettyplot_600() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  remove_map_axes()

plot_grid(
  p1, p2,
  ncol = 1,
  align = "v"
)








Figure 2: Map showing global distribution of total nitrogen and carbon loading for the MD feed at mean digestibility.





Figure 3 maps the spatial distribution of C and N loading for the MD feed at mean digestibility side by side.


Prepare global loading difference data comparing MD to PD and AD feeds
global_data_all <- rbind(farm_ends_carbon, farm_ends_nitrogen) %>%
  merge(farm_locs, by = "farm_ID") %>% 
  st_as_sf(crs = 4326) %>% 
  st_transform(crs = "+proj=robin") %>% 
  select(farm_ID, feed, feed_level, nutrient, mean_value_perbiom)

global_data_PD_diff <- global_data_all %>% 
  filter(feed %in% c("MD", "PD") & feed_level == "mean") %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = feed, values_from = mean_value_perbiom) %>% 
  mutate(diff = PD - MD) %>% 
  select(-MD, -PD)

global_data_AD_diff <- global_data_all %>% 
  filter(feed %in% c("MD", "AD") & feed_level == "mean") %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = feed, values_from = mean_value_perbiom) %>% 
  mutate(diff = AD - MD) %>% 
  select(-MD, -AD)




Map global C and N loading for MD feed (side-by-side)
plot_grid(
  p1, p2,
  ncol = 2,
  align = "v"
)








Figure 3: Map showing global distribution of total carbon and nitrogen loading for the MD feed at mean digestibility.

Figure 8 shows how the change in nutrient loading when switching from MD feed to either PD or AD varies with absolute latitude across all farm locations globally.


Plot change in C and N loading vs absolute latitude when switching from MD to PD or AD feed
p1 <- rbind(farm_ends_carbon, farm_ends_nitrogen) %>% 
  filter(feed_level == "mean") %>% 
  left_join(farm_locs, by = join_by(farm_ID)) %>% 
  mutate(latitute = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2]) %>% 
  select(farm_ID, feed, nutrient, mean_value_perbiom, feed_level, country, latitude) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = feed, values_from = mean_value_perbiom) %>% 
  mutate(
    MD_PD_shift = PD - MD,
    MD_AD_shift = AD - MD
  ) %>% 
  select(-contains("biomar")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = contains("shift"),
    names_to = "shift_to", 
    values_to = "change_mean_perbiomass"
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    shift_to = factor(shift_to, 
      levels = c("MD_PD_shift", "MD_AD_shift"),
      labels = c("PD", "AD")),
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, 
      levels = c("C", "N"),
      labels = c("Carbon", "Nitrogen")),
    ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(
    x = abs(latitude), 
    y = drop_units(change_mean_perbiomass), 
    colour = shift_to
  )) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = feed_pal) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 12, 2), limits = c(0,12)) +
  facet_wrap(~nutrient) +
  prettyplot_600() +
  labs(
    x = expression("Aboslute latitude ("*degree*")"),
    y = expression("Change in loading (kg"~t[fish]^{-1}*")")
  )

legend_grob <- ggpubr::get_legend(
  p1 +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(ncol = 1)) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey70", linewidth = 0.3),
      legend.margin     = margin(5, 6, 5, 6),
      legend.key.size   = unit(0.4, "cm"),
      legend.text       = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.title      = element_blank()
    )
)

p1 + inset_element(
  legend_grob,
  left   = 0.85, right  = 0.95,  # x start, x end (fraction of plot width)
  bottom = 0.85, top    = 0.95,   # y start, y end
  align_to = "full"  # relative to full plot, not just panel area
)








Figure 8: Change in nutrient loading when changing from MD to PD (green) or from MD to AD (orange) at all farm locations globally.

text:
3.4 Tradeoff plot


Create ternary tradeoff plot of C loading, N loading, and biomass production
weight_data <- file.path(output_path, "model_run_outputs", "fish_ends_weight.qs") %>% 
  qread() %>% 
  group_by(farm_ID, feed) %>% 
  reframe(mean_value = mean(value)) %>% 
  convert_feeds_to_levels() %>% 
  mutate(
    farm_ID = as.integer(farm_ID),
    feed = factor(feed, 
      levels = c("marine_dominant_biomar", "plant_dominant_biomar", "animal_dominant_biomar"),
      labels = c("MD", "PD", "AD")
      )
    )

plt_data <- farm_ends_carbon %>%
  select(farm_ID, feed, feed_level, carbon_perbiom = mean_value_perbiom) %>%
  inner_join(
    farm_ends_nitrogen %>% select(farm_ID, feed, feed_level, nitrogen_perbiom = mean_value_perbiom),
    by = c("farm_ID", "feed", "feed_level")
  ) %>%
  inner_join(
    weight_data %>% select(farm_ID, feed, feed_level, biomass = mean_value),
    by = c("farm_ID", "feed", "feed_level")
  ) %>%
  # Step 1: min-max scale each dimension to [0, 1] so columns are comparable.
  mutate(
    c = rescale(drop_units(carbon_perbiom)),
    n = rescale(drop_units(nitrogen_perbiom)),
    b = rescale(biomass),
    feed_level = factor(
      feed_level,
      levels = c("min", "mean", "max"),
      labels = c("Minimum", "Meanimum", "Maximum")
    )
  )

text:
4.2 Daily values


Load and summarise daily C and N loading by country
farm_runs <- map2_dfr(
  c(file.path(output_path, "model_run_outputs", "farm_runs_total_carbon.qs"),
    file.path(output_path, "model_run_outputs", "farm_runs_total_nitrogen.qs")),
  c("C", "N"),
  function(fnm, nutr) {
    fnm %>% 
      qread() %>% 
      clean_up() %>% 
      filter(feed == "MD" & feed_level == "mean") %>% 
      mutate(nutrient = as.factor(nutr))
    }
  ) %>% 
  left_join(farm_locs, by = join_by(farm_ID))

# How many farms per country? 10 seems like enough
farm_runs <- farm_runs %>% 
  group_by(country, prod_t, nutrient) %>% 
  reframe(
    n = n(),
    mean_value_perbiom = mean(mean_value_perbiom),
    sd_per_biom = sqrt(mean(sd_per_biom^2))

title: 6: Assigning production
text:
← Previous: Formulating experimental feeds

1 Overview
The purpose of this document is to choose which farms will be used to grow salmon and assign each of those farms a “production quota”.


2 Get data
Get production and farm location data. This is grouped by country and FAO code (not farm).


Load production and farm location data
mariculture_prod_sal <- file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "mariculture_prod_salmonids.qs") %>% 
  qread() %>% 
  filter(model_name == "atlantic_salmon")

farms_w_temp_df <- file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "suitable_salmonid_locations_w_temps.qs") %>% 
  qread()

farm_list <- farms_w_temp_df %>% 
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  distinct(iso3c, country, species_group, fao_code, hemisphere, rownum) 
farm_list <- farm_list %>% 
  group_by(iso3c, country, fao_code) %>% 
  group_split()




3 Assign production to farms
What this does across each country, species group, and fishing region:

Counts the estimated number of farms (from mapping data)
Pulls the correct (matching) production data, groups by model_name (NOT farm_name), and estimates the number of farms that needs to be assigned to that model_name (based on proportionate salmonid production)
Assigns that model_name to a selection of farms in that country/region



Assign model species and production quotas to individual farm locations
# For testing
# this_country_region <- farm_list[[3]]

map_spp_to_locations_list <- map(
  farm_list, 
  function(this_country_region){
    # Determine which region we're looking at
    this_iso3c <- this_country_region$iso3c %>% unique()
    this_fao_code <- this_country_region$fao_code %>% unique()
    this_species_group <- this_country_region$species_group %>% unique()
    
    # Count how many farms need to be in that region (all salmonids)
    this_n_farms <- this_country_region %>% nrow()

    # Get the matching production data and calculate what proportion of national salmonid production should go to each model_name
    this_country_region_spp_production_list <- mariculture_prod_sal %>% 
      filter(
        ISO3_Code == this_iso3c, 
        farm_name == this_species_group, 
        fao_code == this_fao_code
        ) %>% 
      group_by(model_name, nat_prod_sal) %>% 
      reframe(production = sum(production)) %>% 
      mutate(prop_production = drop_units(production/nat_prod_sal)) %>% 
      distinct() %>% 
      group_by(model_name) %>% 
      group_split()

    # For testing:
    # this_species_prop <- this_country_region_spp_production_list[[1]]
    
    # For each model name is the country/fishing region, assign a random selection of farms to it (matching its proportionate production)
    farm_locations_w_model_spp <- map_df(
      this_country_region_spp_production_list, 
      function(this_species_prop){
        
        this_spp_n_farms <- round(this_n_farms * this_species_prop$prop_production)
        
        set.seed(1234)
        indices <- sample(nrow(this_country_region), size = this_spp_n_farms)
          
        this_spp_locations <- this_country_region[indices,]
        this_spp_locations$model_name <- this_species_prop$model_name

        this_country_region <- this_country_region[-indices,]
      
        return(this_spp_locations)
    })
    return(farm_locations_w_model_spp)
}, .progress = T
)

map_spp_to_locations <- lapply(
    map_spp_to_locations_list, 
    FUN = function(element) if(nrow(element) == 0) NULL else element
  ) %>% 
  bind_rows() %>% # removes the NULL record returns
  mutate(farm_id = row_number())

farm_static_data <- farms_w_temp_df %>% 
  group_by(rownum) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 1) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(rownum, geometry) %>% 
  merge(map_spp_to_locations, by = "rownum", all = F) %>% 
  select(-rownum)

farm_temporal_data <- farms_w_temp_df %>% 
  st_drop_geometry() %>% 
  select(rownum, day, temp_c) %>% 
  merge(map_spp_to_locations, by = c("rownum"), all = F) %>% 
  select(-rownum)

qsave(farm_static_data, file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_static_data.qs"))
qsave(farm_temporal_data, file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_temporal_data.qs"))




Map assigned farm locations by country
p_bigmap_mercator +
  geom_sf(
    data = farm_static_data, 
    aes(color = country, geometry = geometry), 
    size = 2
  ) +
  coord_sf() +
  prettyplot_300()




4 Species and population parameters


Load species-specific parameters from Excel file
species_params <- readxl::read_excel(
  path = c(file = file.path(input_species_param_path, "Species.xlsx"), sheet = "Atlantic salmon")["file"], 
  sheet = c(file = file.path(input_species_param_path, "Species.xlsx"), sheet = "Atlantic salmon")["sheet"]
)
vals <- species_params$Value
names(vals) <- species_params$Quantity
species_params <- vals[!is.na(vals)]
qsave(species_params, file.path(output_species_data_path, "species_params.qs"))




Load population-specific parameters
pop_params <- readxl::read_excel(path = c(file = file.path(input_species_param_path, "Population.xlsx"))["file"])
vals <- pop_params$Value
names(vals) <- pop_params$Quantity
pop_params <- vals[!is.na(vals)]
qsave(pop_params, file.path(output_species_data_path, "pop_params.qs"))




5 Grow salmon on default feed
Grow salmon at each location using the default feed and the extracted temperature data.


Load model parameters and farm data
feed_params <- file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_PD.qs") %>% qread()

all_params <- c(
  file.path(output_species_data_path, "species_params.qs") %>% qread(),
  file.path(output_species_data_path, "pop_params.qs") %>% qread()
)

times_N <- c("t_start" = 121, "t_end" = 121+547, "dt" = 1)
times_S <- c("t_start" = 274, "t_end" = 274+547, "dt" = 1)

farm_static_data <- file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_static_data.qs") %>% qread()
farm_temporal_data <- file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_temporal_data.qs") %>% qread()

farm_data <- farm_temporal_data %>% 
  mutate(
    t_start = case_when(hemisphere == "N" ~ times_N['t_start'], TRUE ~ times_S['t_start']) %>% unname() %>% as.integer(), 
    t_end = case_when(hemisphere == "N" ~ times_N['t_end'], TRUE ~ times_S['t_end']) %>% unname() %>% as.integer(),
    ) %>% 
    filter(day >= t_start & day <= t_end) %>% 
    group_by(farm_id) %>% 
    group_split()


Get a representative harvest size for each farm.


Run fish growth model at each farm location to determine harvest size
# For testing
# farm_data <- farm_data[1:50]

# For testing:
# temp <- farm_data[[106]]

plan(multisession)
harvest_size <- futuremap_dfr(
  farm_data, 
  function(temp) {
    # Grow "ideal" fish
    fg <- fish_growth(
      species_params = all_params,
      water_temp = temp$temp_c,
      feed_params = feed_params[["plant_dominant_biomar"]],
      times = c(t_start = min(temp$day), t_end = max(temp$day), dt = 1),
      init_weight = all_params["meanW"],
      ingmax = all_params["meanImax"]
    ) %>%
      as.data.frame() %>% 
      mutate(farm_ID = unique(temp$farm_id))

    # Slice a "harvest window" and pick best time
    fg %>% 
      slice_tail(n = 60) %>% 
      filter(!is.na(weight)) %>% 
      slice_max(weight) %>% 
      select(farm_ID, days, weight)
  },
  .progress = T,
  .options = future_options(seed = TRUE)
)
plan(paralell)

qsave(harvest_size, file.path(gendata_path, "harvest_sizes", "farm_harvest_sizes.qs"))


View harvest size.


Plot distribution of harvest sizes and their spatial distribution
harvest_size <- qread(file.path(gendata_path, "harvest_sizes", "farm_harvest_sizes.qs"))

harvest_size %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = weight)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 250, colour = "black", fill = "salmon", alpha = 0.35) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(x = "Individual harvest weight", y = "Frequency")

p_bigmap_mercator +
  geom_sf(
    data = full_join(farm_static_data, harvest_size, by = c("farm_id" = "farm_ID")), 
    aes(color = weight, geometry = geometry), 
    size = 2
  ) +
  coord_sf() +
  prettyplot_300() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")




6 Assign stocking population
Production needs to be assigned based on what the farm can actually handle. A country’s production is the sum of all its farms harvest size * stocking population. For the sake of simplicity, lets assume that each country/fao_code uses the same stocking population in each of its farms. Therefore, we need to find the correct stocking density (population) for the farms in each country so that it will meet its projected production.
\[
Prod_{national} = \sum{pop_{national} * indsize_{farm}}
\]


Calculate the required salmon population (per farm, country-specific) required to meet country-level production based on the individual harvest size they can achieve.
combs <- farm_static_data %>% distinct(iso3c, fao_code)

farm_static_data <- file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_static_data.qs") %>% 
  qread() %>% 
  full_join(harvest_size, by = c("farm_id" = "farm_ID")) %>% 
  mutate(harvest_size = set_units(weight, "g"))

# For testing
# c <- 9

stocking_info <- map_dfr(
  1:nrow(combs),
  function(c) {
    country_farm_static_data <- farm_static_data %>% 
      filter(iso3c == combs$iso3c[c] & fao_code == combs$fao_code[c])

    country_prod_data <- mariculture_prod_sal %>% 
      filter(ISO3_Code == combs$iso3c[c] & fao_code == combs$fao_code[c])

    harvest_n <- drop_units(sum(country_prod_data$nat_prod_sal/country_farm_static_data$harvest_size))
    country_farm_static_data$harvest_n <- harvest_n

    # for some reason start and end times got lost
    country_farm_static_data %>% 
      mutate(
        t_start = case_when(
          hemisphere == "N" ~ times_N['t_start'], 
          TRUE ~ times_S['t_start']
          ) %>% unname() %>% as.integer(), 
        t_end = case_when(
          hemisphere == "N" ~ times_N['t_end'], 
          TRUE ~ times_S['t_end']
          ) %>% unname() %>% as.integer()
      )
  }
)

qsave(stocking_info, file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_static_data_w_stocking.qs"))

title: 4: Extracting farm temperature data
text:
← Previous: Assessing species potential

1 Overview
The purpose of this document is to extract daily temperature timeseries from SST data to drive fish growth in the correct places. The steps are:

Pull farm locations data (previously determined from FAO stocking locations)
Record whether the farm is in the southern or northern hemisphere (determines cohort stocking date)
Pull SST data from .tif file and construct “typical” temperature year for each farm location
Gapfill missing temperature data using closest farm in the same FAO stocking area
Save temperature data both with and without geometry information
Determine which farms have a mean temperature <=6 C (will be omitted)



2 Farm locations
Get global farm locations and determine whether they’re in the southern or northern hemisphere.


Get all farm locations, determine hemisphere
farms <- here(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_locations_w_fishing_areas.qs") %>% 
  qread() %>% 
  mutate(
    latitude = sf::st_coordinates(geometry)[,2],
    hemisphere = case_when(latitude > 0 ~ "N", TRUE ~ "S")
  ) %>% 
  select(-latitude) %>% 
  mutate(rownum = row_number())


Get daily temperatures for a typical year.


Create typical year of SST for all farms
day_number <- seq(1:365)

# For testing:
# dn <- 100

temp_data <- purrr::map_dfc(
  day_number, 
  function(dn){  
    sst_test <- file.path(input_farm_sst_path, "SST_gf_rasters", sprintf("sst_nasa_mur_L4_0.25_mean_2017_2022_day_%s.tif", fixnum(dn, 3))) %>% 
      terra::rast()
    # plot(sst_test)

    # Extract with simple method first
    sst_simple <- terra::extract(sst_test, farms, method = "simple", ID = F) %>% 
      rename(simple = focal_mean)

    # Extract with bilinear for comparison
    sst_bilinear <- terra::extract(sst_test, farms, method = "bilinear") %>% 
      rename(bilinear = focal_mean)

    # Use simple where available, bilinear where NA
    sst_simple %>% 
      cbind(sst_bilinear) %>% 
      mutate(day = paste0("day_", dn)) %>% 
      mutate(focal_mean = case_when(is.na(simple)~bilinear, T ~ simple)) %>% 
      select(-c(simple, bilinear)) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = "day", values_from = "focal_mean") %>% 
      select(-ID)
  },
  .progress = T
) %>%
  mutate(rownum = row_number())

farms_w_temp_df <- farms %>%
  left_join(temp_data, by = c("rownum" = "rownum")) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("day_"),
    names_to = "day", names_prefix = "day_", names_transform = list(day = as.integer),
    values_to = "temp_c"
  )


Check which farms have missing temp data. The above method already tries bilinear interpretation, so this will need something a little more custom.


Check which farms have missing temp data
(
  missing_temp_farms <- farms_w_temp_df %>% 
    filter(temp_c %>% is.na()) %>% 
    group_by(rownum) %>% 
    reframe(num_missing = n())
)
missing_temp_farms <- farms %>% 
  filter(rownum %in% unique(missing_temp_farms$rownum))
# There are just 2 farms missing data

day_number = c(1:365)
temp_data <- purrr::map_dfc(
  day_number, 
  function(dn){
    sst_test <- file.path(input_farm_sst_path, "SST_gf_rasters", sprintf("sst_nasa_mur_L4_0.25_mean_2017_2022_day_%s.tif", fixnum(dn, 3))) %>% 
      terra::rast()
    # plot(sst_test)

    # Get cell numbers for each point
    cell_nums <- terra::cellFromXY(sst_test, terra::crds(terra::vect(missing_temp_farms)))

    # Get adjacent cells (includes the 16 surrounding cells)
    adj_cells <- sst_values <- terra::adjacent(sst_test, cell_nums, directions = 16)
    for (r in 1:nrow(adj_cells)) {
      sst_values[r, ] <- sst_test[adj_cells[r, ]]$focal_mean
    }

    data.frame(
      ID = 1:nrow(sst_values),
      focal_mean = rowMeans(sst_values, na.rm = TRUE)
      ) %>% 
      mutate(day = paste0("day_", dn)) %>% 
      pivot_wider(names_from = "day", values_from = "focal_mean") %>% 
      select(-ID)
  },
  .progress = T
) %>%
  mutate(rownum = missing_temp_farms$rownum)

farms_w_temp_df_2 <- farms %>%
  merge(temp_data, by = c("rownum" = "rownum"), all.x = F, all.y = T) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("day_"),
    names_to = "day", names_prefix = "day_", names_transform = list(day = as.integer),
    values_to = "temp_c"
  ) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(temp_c))

farms_w_temp_df <- farms_w_temp_df %>% 
  filter(!rownum %in% farms_w_temp_df_2$rownum) %>% 
  rbind(farms_w_temp_df_2)




Save data
# Create three identical years
farms_w_temp_df <- rbind(
  farms_w_temp_df,
  farms_w_temp_df %>% 
    mutate(day = day + 365),
  farms_w_temp_df %>% 
    mutate(day = day + 365 + 365)
)

qsave(farms_w_temp_df, file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "possible_salmonid_locations_w_temps.qs"))




3 Mean farm temperatures
Now get overall mean temperatures of farms to exclude those where mean temp < 6 degrees C.


Get mean temperature of farms
mean_temps <- farms_w_temp_df %>% 
  group_by(hemisphere, country, fao_code, rownum) %>% 
  reframe(
    mean_temp = mean(temp_c),
    sd_temp = sd(temp_c)
    )

mean_temps %>% 
  mutate(too_cold = case_when(mean_temp < 6 ~ 1, T ~ 0)) %>% 
  group_by(hemisphere, country, fao_code) %>% 
  reframe(
    total_sites = n(),
    too_cold = sum(too_cold),
    prop_too_cold = too_cold/total_sites
  )

cold_farms <- mean_temps$rownum[mean_temps$mean_temp < 6]


This doesn’t mean that those farms are the most suited to growing salmon (their temperatures can still dip below 6 degrees).


Plot temperature timeseries by country and FAO region
farms_w_temp_df %>%
  filter(day <= 365 & !rownum %in% cold_farms) %>% 
  group_by(hemisphere, country, fao_code, day) %>% 
  reframe(
    mean_temp = mean(temp_c),
    sd_temp = sd(temp_c)
  ) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(
    x = day, y = mean_temp, ymin = mean_temp-sd_temp, ymax = mean_temp+sd_temp,
    colour = interaction(fao_code, country), fill = interaction(fao_code, country)
    )) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_ribbon(alpha = 0.25) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(hemisphere)) +
  prettyplot_300() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")


This data is saved as “suitable” salmon locations. Individual farms are assigned production in the next step.


Save suitable farm locations with temperature data
qsave(
  farms_w_temp_df %>% filter(!rownum %in% cold_farms),
  file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "suitable_salmonid_locations_w_temps.qs")
)


Next: Formulating experimental feeds →

Add FAO fishing area codes to production data
mariculture_prod_all <- mariculture_prod_all %>% 
  mutate(farm_name = "Salmonidae fish") %>% # to match with farm location names
  left_join(FAO_code_key, by = c("fao_fishing_area" = "NAME_EN")) %>%

title: Local-to-Global Mariculture Modelling
text:
All code and processes for this project are linked below. Note that some scripts require access to raw data which is not stored on Github.





Page
Description




Tidying data
Tidies the raw input data needed for the analysis. Processes FAO aquaculture production data, cleans farm location data for salmonids, downloads and processes daily SST rasters, and loads species vulnerability and distribution data.


Extracting production locations
Matches salmonid farm locations to FAO fishing regions and country-level production data, checks that the spatial matching is consistent, and assigns model species to individual farms.


Assessing species potential
Assesses the global production potential of candidate marine finfish species using FAO aquaculture statistics, identifying which species and countries dominate production and selecting the focus species for the modelling work.


Extracting farm temperature data
Extracts daily temperature timeseries from SST rasters to drive fish growth at each farm location, gapfills missing values, and constructs a “typical year” for model inputs.


Formulating experimental feeds
Formulates all the feeds (ingredient compositions, macronutrient profiles) that will be used in the model runs.


Assigning Production
Chooses which farms will be used to grow salmon and assigns each farm a production quota, including calculating typical harvest size and population per farm to meet national production targets.


How many runs per farm?
Determines how many Monte-Carlo simulation runs per farm are sufficient for acceptable variation in model outputs.


Run Atlantic salmon model
Runs the bioenergetics aquaculture model for Atlantic salmon across all farm locations and feed scenarios.









Page
Description




Data analysis
Performs additional data analysis on model outputs, including fitting mixed-effects models for variance partitioning across feeds, farms and individual fish, and deriving key numbers for the manuscript.


Figures for manuscript
Generates all main and supplementary figures used in the final manuscript.

title: 1: Tidying data
text:
← Previous: Home

1 Overview
This document tidies the raw input data needed for the analysis. It processes FAO aquaculture production data, cleans farm location data for salmonids, downloads and processes daily SST rasters, and loads species vulnerability and distribution data.


Define species of interest
# Specify species of interest
species <- c("atlantic_salmon", "general_salmonid")




2 Aquaculture production
Clean aquaculture production data from the FAO.


Load and clean raw FAO aquaculture quantity data
# Get aquaculture quantity in tonnes live weight
quantity <- file.path(bigdata_path, "Aquaculture_2025.1.0", "Aquaculture_Quantity.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  clean_names() %>% 
  mutate(value = set_units(value, "t")) %>% 
  select(-measure)

# Apply environment codes and filter by "marine"
environment_codes <- file.path(bigdata_path, "Aquaculture_2025.1.0", "CL_FI_PRODENVIRONMENT.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  distinct(Code, Name_En)

quantity <- quantity %>% 
  merge(environment_codes, by.x = "environment_alpha_2_code", by.y = "Code") %>%
  rename(environment = Name_En) %>% 
  select(-environment_alpha_2_code)

# Apply species codes
species_codes <- file.path(bigdata_path, "Aquaculture_2025.1.0", "CL_FI_SPECIES_GROUPS.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  distinct(X3A_Code, Scientific_Name)

quantity <- quantity %>% 
  merge(species_codes, by.x = "species_alpha_3_code", by.y = "X3A_Code") %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Scientific_Name)) %>% 
  select(-species_alpha_3_code)

# Apply status codes
status_codes <- file.path(bigdata_path, "Aquaculture_2025.1.0", "CL_FI_SYMBOL_SDMX.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  distinct(Symbol, Name_En, Description_En)

quantity <- quantity %>% 
  merge(status_codes, by.x = "status", by.y = "Symbol") %>% 
  rename(status_nm = Name_En,
         status_description = Description_En) %>% 
  select(-status)

# Apply country and area codes
country_codes <- file.path(bigdata_path, "Aquaculture_2025.1.0", "CL_FI_COUNTRY_GROUPS.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  select(UN_Code, Identifier, Name_En, ISO3_Code)
area_codes <- file.path(bigdata_path, "Aquaculture_2025.1.0", "CL_FI_WATERAREA_GROUPS.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  select(Code, Name_En)

quantity <- quantity %>% 
  merge(country_codes, by.x = "country_un_code", by.y = "UN_Code", all.x = T, all.y = F) %>% 
  merge(area_codes, by.x = "area_code", by.y = "Code", all.x = T, all.y = F) %>% 
  rename(
    country = Name_En.x, 
    fao_fishing_area = Name_En.y,
    country_id = Identifier
    ) %>% 
  select(-status_description)

quantity <- quantity %>% 
  mutate(
    environment = as.factor(environment),
    Scientific_Name = as.factor(Scientific_Name),
    status_nm = as.factor(status_nm),
    country = as.factor(country),
    fao_fishing_area = as.factor(fao_fishing_area),
    ISO3_Code = as.factor(ISO3_Code)
  ) %>% 
  filter(
    period > 2000 &
    environment == "Marine" & 
    status_nm != "Not significant (<0.5)"
    )

qsave(quantity, file.path(species_path, "production", "aquaculture_quantity.qs"))




3 Mariculture locations
Pull in mariculture locations data from Clawson et al (2022) and save the species groups of interest.


Read in farm locations and save for salmonids only
farm_locations <- file.path(bigdata_path, "all_marine_aquaculture_farms_sources_final.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  mutate(tonnes_per_farm = set_units(tonnes_per_farm)) %>% 
  filter(species_group == "Salmonidae fish") %>% 
  select(-c(details, data_type_2, data_source, data_type, data_year))

qsave(farm_locations, file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "salmon_marine_farm_locations.qs"))




4 Daily SST data
From NASA - GHRSST Level 4 MUR 0.25deg Global Foundation Sea Surface Temperature Analysis


Take daily means from raw data
SST_files <- file.path(bigdata_path, "NASA/GHRSST_L4_MUR_0.25_SST") %>% 
  list.files(full.names = TRUE, pattern = ".nc")

# this_file <- SST_files[[1]]
# this_year=2013

# Initialise lists for each year
years <- c(2017:2022)
year_lists <- vector(mode ="list", length = length(years))

# Add annual files to a list of files for each year
for(y in 1:length(years)){
  this_year <- years[y]
  this_years_files <- SST_files[grep(paste0(this_year, "_"), SST_files)]
  this_years_files <- this_years_files[!grepl(paste0(this_year, "_0229"), this_years_files)]
  year_lists[[y]] <- this_years_files
}
  
# Extract the daily files for each year and rasterize in a stack
days <- c(1:365)
rast_list = vector(mode = "list", length = length(days))

map(
  days, 
  function(this_day){
    this_days_file_list <- sapply(year_lists, "[[", this_day)
    this_day_stack <- rast(this_days_file_list, subds = "analysed_sst")
    this_day_mean_rast <- app(this_day_stack, "mean", na.rm=TRUE) - 273.15
    writeRaster(
      this_day_mean_rast, 
      filename = file.path(
        input_farm_sst_path, "SST_raw_rasters", sprintf(
        "sst_nasa_mur_L4_0.25_mean_2017_2022_day_%s.tif", fixnum(this_day, digits = 3)
      ))
    )
  },
  .progress = T
)




Gapfill the saved rasters
raw_SST_files <- list.files(file.path(input_farm_sst_path, "SST_raw_rasters"), full.names = TRUE) %>% 
  sort()

# For testing function
# this_file <- raw_SST_files[[1]]

map(
  raw_SST_files, 
  function(this_file){
    saveName <-  basename(this_file)
    this_rast <- rast(this_file)
    this_gf_rast <- focal(this_rast, w = 7, fun = "mean", na.policy = "only")
    writeRaster(
      this_gf_rast, 
      file.path(input_farm_sst_path, "SST_gf_rasters", sprintf("%s", saveName)),
      overwrite = T
    )
  },
  .progress = T
)




5 Vulnerability
Pull in species vulnerability to eutrophication data and save to the salmon folder.


Load species vulnerability to eutrophication data
vuln_scores <- file.path(bigdata_path, "butt_spp_vuln_framework-publication/_output/vuln_gapfilled_score.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  select(vuln_gf_id, eutrophication_nutrient_pollution)

vuln_sd <- file.path(bigdata_path, "butt_spp_vuln_framework-publication/_output/vuln_gapfilled_sd.csv") %>% 
  read.csv() %>% 
  select(vuln_gf_id, eutrophication_nutrient_pollution) %>% 
  rename(sd = eutrophication_nutrient_pollution)

vuln_tx <- file.path(bigdata_path, "butt_spp_vuln_framework-publication/_output/vuln_gapfilled_tx.csv") %>% 
  read.csv()

full_vuln_df <- 
  vuln_scores %>% 
  left_join(vuln_sd) %>% 
  left_join(vuln_tx)

qsave(full_vuln_df, here("data/_general_data/species_layers/vulnerabilities/marine_spp_vulnerabilities_eutrophication.qs"))




6 Distribution
Pull in species distribution data from Aquamaps.


Load species distribution data from Aquamaps
aquamaps_0.5d <- data.table::fread(file.path(rdsi_data_dir, "aquamaps/aquamaps_0.6_depth_prepped.csv")) 

#all cells have an id. These id's can be used to isolate only the cells of relevance for the salmon farming locations.
filter(aquamaps_0.5d, is.na(cell_id))

#save to project
qsave(x = aquamaps_0.5d, file = here("data/_general_data/species_layers/distribution/aquamaps_0.5d.qs"))


Next: Extract production locations →

title: 3: Assessing species potential
5: Formulating experimental feeds
text:
← Previous: Extracting farm temperature data

1 Overview
The purpose of this document is to formulate all the feeds that will be used in the analysis. This needs to be done before the model pipeline is run.


2 Import ingredients
There is no check or correction here to ensure that protein + lipid + ash + carb = 1 in the incoming ingredient data. This needs to be checked manually prior to import.


Get ingredient details (PLC composition, digestibility)
ingreds <- file.path(input_feed_profile_path, "all_ingredients.xlsx") %>% 
  read_excel(sheet = "all_ingredients") %>% 
  mutate(ingredient = as.factor(ingredient))

ingred_nms <- levels(ingreds$ingredient)




3 Import feeds
Unlike ingredients, the feeds do not need to be checked for sum = 1 prior to import. That check is done here.


Feed compositions
feed_inputs <- file.path(input_feed_profile_path, "all_feeds.xlsx") %>% 
  read_excel(sheet = "all_feeds")
  
feed_inputs <- feed_inputs %>% 
  pivot_longer(names_to = "feed", values_to = "proportion", cols = !contains(c("ingredient", "category"))) %>% 
  mutate(
    feed = as.factor(feed),
    ingredient = as.factor(ingredient),
    proportion = case_when(is.na(proportion) ~ 0, T ~ proportion)
  ) %>% 
  left_join(ingreds, by = "ingredient")


Check that ingredient proportions sum to 1.


Check composition proportions sum to 1
# Any missing ingredients?
feed_inputs %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(feed, regex("original", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>% 
  filter(str_detect(feed, regex("biomar", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>% 
  filter(proportion != 0 & is.na(protein)) %>% 
  pull(ingredient)

# Sum ingredients to 1
feed_inputs <- feed_inputs %>%
  filter(!str_detect(feed, regex("original", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(proportion) & proportion != 0 & str_detect(feed, regex("biomar", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>%
  group_by(feed) %>% 
  mutate(
    sum = sum(proportion),
    proportion = proportion/sum
  ) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(
    ingredient = droplevels(ingredient),
    feed = droplevels(feed)
  ) %>% 
  select(-c(sum, ash, in_use, in_MS, exp_data, `SUM CHECK`))




4 Prepare for pipeline
The following chunk allows as many ingredients and feeds to be added to the incoming data as needed, it will still be formatted into a form that the targets pipelines can use.


Format feeds as lists to give to targets
feed_types <- levels(feed_inputs$feed)

feed_params_all <- purrr::map(feed_types, function(ft) {
  # Filter by specified feed
  df <- feed_inputs %>% filter(feed == ft) 

  list(
    Proteins = df %>% 
      select(ingredient, proportion, contains("protein")) %>% 
      rename(
        macro = protein, 
        mean_digest = mean_protein_digestibility,
        min_digest = min_protein_digestibility,
        max_digest = max_protein_digestibility
      ),
    Carbohydrates = df %>% 
      select(ingredient, proportion, contains("carb")) %>% 
      rename(
        macro = carb, 
        mean_digest = mean_carb_digestibility,
        min_digest = min_carb_digestibility,
        max_digest = max_carb_digestibility
      ),
    Lipids = df %>% 
      select(ingredient, proportion, contains("lipid")) %>% 
      rename(
        macro = lipid, 
        mean_digest = mean_lipid_digestibility,
        min_digest = min_lipid_digestibility,
        max_digest = max_lipid_digestibility
      )
  )
}) %>% 
  setNames(feed_types)


This next chunk creates the mean, minimum and maximum digestibility variants of each of the feeds.


Split into mean, min, max digestibility
# Functions
make_mean_digest <- function(df) {
  df %>% 
    mutate(digest = mean_digest) %>% 
    select(ingredient, proportion, macro, digest)
}
make_min_digest <- function(df) {
  df %>% 
    mutate(digest = min_digest) %>% 
    select(ingredient, proportion, macro, digest)
}
make_max_digest <- function(df) {
  df %>% 
    mutate(digest = max_digest) %>% 
    select(ingredient, proportion, macro, digest)
}

feed_params <- list(
  "marine_dominant_biomar"     = feed_params_all[["marine_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_mean_digest),
  "marine_dominant_biomar_min" = feed_params_all[["marine_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_min_digest),
  "marine_dominant_biomar_max" = feed_params_all[["marine_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_max_digest),
  "animal_dominant_biomar"     = feed_params_all[["animal_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_mean_digest),
  "animal_dominant_biomar_min" = feed_params_all[["animal_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_min_digest),
  "animal_dominant_biomar_max" = feed_params_all[["animal_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_max_digest),
  "plant_dominant_biomar"     = feed_params_all[["plant_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_mean_digest),
  "plant_dominant_biomar_min" = feed_params_all[["plant_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_min_digest),
  "plant_dominant_biomar_max" = feed_params_all[["plant_dominant_biomar"]] %>% lapply(make_max_digest)
  )




Save feed params file
qsave(feed_params, file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_all.qs"))

feed_params_MD <- feed_params[1:3]
feed_params_PD <- feed_params[7:9]
feed_params_AD <- feed_params[4:6]

qsave(feed_params_MD, file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_MD.qs"))
qsave(feed_params_PD, file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_PD.qs"))
qsave(feed_params_AD, file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_AD.qs"))




5 Exploring C/N


Explore the top lipid-providing ingredients across feeds
feed_params_MD <- qread(file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_MD.qs"))
feed_params_PD <- qread(file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_PD.qs"))
feed_params_AD <- qread(file.path(output_species_data_path, "feed_params_AD.qs"))
feed_params <- c(feed_params_MD, feed_params_PD, feed_params_AD)

# Look at the most important lipid ingredients
rbind(
  feed_params_MD[[1]][["Lipids"]] %>% mutate(total = proportion * macro),
  feed_params_PD[[1]][["Lipids"]] %>% mutate(total = proportion * macro),
  feed_params_AD[[1]][["Lipids"]] %>% mutate(total = proportion * macro)
  ) %>% 
    group_by(ingredient) %>% 
    reframe(total = sum(total)) %>% 
    arrange(-total)

# These are the top ten lipid-providing ingredients in the feeds.
# canola-oil
# fish-oil-forage
# poultry-oil
# algal-oil
# fish-oil-trimmings
# fishmeal-forage
# poultry-meal
# fishmeal-trimmings
# wheat
# soy-protein-concentrate




6 Feed information


Calculate total macronutrient content of each feed
proteins <- feed_params %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls$Proteins %>% 
      mutate(prop_macro = proportion * macro) %>% 
      pull(prop_macro) %>% sum()
  }) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = names(feed_params), names_to = "feed", values_to = "protein")

lipids <- feed_params %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls$Lipids %>% 
      mutate(prop_macro = proportion * macro) %>% 
      pull(prop_macro) %>% sum()
  }) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = names(feed_params), names_to = "feed", values_to = "lipid")

carbs <- feed_params %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls$Carbohydrates %>% 
      mutate(prop_macro = proportion * macro) %>% 
      pull(prop_macro) %>% sum()
  }) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = names(feed_params), names_to = "feed", values_to = "carbohydrate")

proteins %>% 
  left_join(lipids, by = "feed") %>% 
  left_join(carbs, by = "feed") %>% 
  mutate(total = protein+lipid+carbohydrate)




Calculate apparent digestibility of each macronutrient per feed
proteins <- feed_params %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls$Proteins %>% 
      filter(macro > 0) %>% 
      mutate(prop_digest = proportion * digest / sum(proportion)) %>% 
      pull(prop_digest) %>% sum()
  }) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = names(feed_params), names_to = "feed", values_to = "AD_protein")

lipids <- feed_params %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls$Lipids %>% 
      filter(macro > 0) %>% 
      mutate(prop_digest = proportion * digest / sum(proportion)) %>% 
      pull(prop_digest) %>% sum()
  }) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = names(feed_params), names_to = "feed", values_to = "AD_lipid")

carbs <- feed_params %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls$Carbohydrates %>% 
      filter(macro > 0) %>% 
      mutate(prop_digest = proportion * digest / sum(proportion)) %>% 
      pull(prop_digest) %>% sum()
  }) %>% 
    as.data.frame() %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = names(feed_params), names_to = "feed", values_to = "AD_carbohydrate")

proteins %>% 
  left_join(lipids, by = "feed") %>% 
  left_join(carbs, by = "feed")




7 Comparing feeds
The three “biomar” feeds were designed to be isoenergetic.


Calculate energy stats about the feeds
feed_params[[1]] %>% 
  lapply(function(ls) {
    ls %>% mutate(total = proportion * macro * digest) %>% 
      pull(total) %>% sum()
  }) %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
    mutate(
      Proteins = Proteins * 23.6,
      Carbohydrates = Carbohydrates * 17.2,
      Lipids = Lipids * 36.2,
      total = Proteins + Carbohydrates + Lipids
    )


feed_stats <- 
  map2(feed_params, names(feed_params), function(ls, feedname) {
    map2(ls, names(ls), function(df, macroname) {
      df %>% mutate(component = macroname)
    }) %>% bind_rows() %>% 
      mutate(feed = feedname)
  }) %>% 
  bind_rows() %>% 
  mutate(
    ing_macro = proportion * macro,
    digest_macro = ing_macro * digest,
    E = case_when(
      component == "Proteins" ~ digest_macro * 23.6,
      component == "Lipids" ~ digest_macro * 36.2,
      T ~ digest_macro * 17.2
    )
  ) %>% 
  group_by(feed) %>% 
  mutate(total_E = sum(E)) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  group_by(feed, component, total_E) %>% 
  reframe(comp_E = sum(E)) %>% 
  mutate(prop_E = comp_E/total_E)

feed_stats %>% 
  group_by(feed) %>%

title: 9: Data analysis
text:
← Previous: Run Atlantic salmon model

1 Overview
This document does some additional data analysis on the outputs before presenting numbers needed for the manuscript. In particular, we fit a series of mixed-effects models to determine how much of the variance can be attributed to fixed effects (different feeds), random effects (different farms) and residual effects (Monte-Carlo sampled individual fish).


Source R scripts and set up custom units
here("src") %>%
  list.files(pattern = "\\.R$", full.names = TRUE) %>%
  str_subset("pipeline", negate = T) %>%
  walk(source)

remove_unit("g_fish")
remove_unit("kg_fish")
remove_unit("t_fish")
install_unit("g_fish")
install_unit("kg_fish", "1000 g_fish")
install_unit("t_fish", "1000 kg_fish")




Get locations of farms for mapping
farm_locs <- file.path(input_farm_coords_path, "farm_static_data.qs") %>% 
  qread() %>% 
  rename(farm_ID = farm_id) %>%
  mutate(
    longitude = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 1],
    latitude = st_coordinates(geometry)[, 2]
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    country = as.factor(country),
    region = case_when(
      country %in% c("Canada", "United States of America") & longitude < -100 ~ "North America (WC)", 
      country %in% c("Canada", "United States of America") & longitude < -50 ~ "North America (EC)", 
      country %in% c("Ireland", "United Kingdom") ~ "UK & Ireland",
      country %in% c("Iceland", "Faroe Islands") ~ "Iceland & FI",
      country %in% c("Norway", "Finland") ~ "Scandinavia",
      country == "Australia" ~ "Australia",
      country == "Chile" ~ "Chile", 
      country == "Russian Federation" ~ NA
    ) %>% as.factor()
  )

Successively removing random effects shows that farm_ID is the only one that really matters wihtout overcomplicating the model structure. In the final model:

Feed and nested feed_level effects are fixed effects
Farm_ID is controlled as a random intercept
The residual represents individual fish variation



Fit actual model for total_nitrogen
VP_nitrogen <- file.path(data_analysis_path, "VP_modelfinal_total_nitrogen.qs")
if (!file.exists(VP_nitrogen)) {
  VP_nitrogen <- lmer(
    value_perbiom ~ feed/feed_level + (1 | farm_ID), 
    data = fish_ends_nitrogen, REML = TRUE,
    control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
  )
  qsave(VP_nitrogen, file.path(data_analysis_path, "VP_modelfinal_total_nitrogen.qs"))
} else {
  VP_nitrogen <- qread(VP_nitrogen)
}

# Random effects variance (location + residual)
vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(VP_nitrogen))
vc$pct <- vc$vcov / sum(vc$vcov) * 100
vc


       grp        var1 var2         vcov        sdcor       pct
1  farm_ID (Intercept) <NA> 3.440420e-07 0.0005865510 91.900049
2 Residual        <NA> <NA> 3.032342e-08 0.0001741362  8.099951


Fit actual model for total_nitrogen
# Fixed effects variance — how much do feed and variant explain?
# Use r.squaredGLMM for marginal (fixed, R2m) vs conditional (fixed + random, R2c) R²
r2 <- as.data.frame(r.squaredGLMM(VP_nitrogen))
r2$farm_ID <- r2$R2c - r2$R2m
r2$ind_fish <- 1 - r2$R2c
r2*100


       R2m     R2c  farm_ID  ind_fish
1 94.73336 99.5734 4.840046 0.4265954

From Julia: Can we say With mean digestibolities there is X% more carbon/nitrogen loading per ton of fish produced per degree (either lat or temperature) for the reference MD feed.     sd_perbiom = sqrt(sum(sd_per_biom^2)) * mean_perbiom
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    mean_perbiom = mean_perbiom %>% set_units("g g_fish-1") %>% set_units("kg t_fish-1") %>% round(2),
    sd_perbiom = sd_perbiom %>% set_units("g g_fish-1") %>% set_units("kg t_fish-1") %>% round(2)
  ) %>% 
  gt()
